explanationZhTw: 表示「再一次、再次」。這裡是很自然的副詞片語，用來說明這件事又發生了一次。
generatedExample: We should check the map once again before we leave.

explanationZhTw: 指「藏身處、隱匿地點」，常用來說秘密藏起來或暫時躲避的地方。
generatedExample: The old cabin became their hide-out during the storm.

explanationZhTw: 表示「當然、自然地」，在這裡帶有說話者覺得這件事很明顯的語氣。
generatedExample: Of course, you can borrow my umbrella.

explanationZhTw: 舉止、風度，指一個人的外在表現和態度。
generatedExample: His demeanour changed the moment the interview began.

explanationZhTw: 有條理的；務實有效率的，像在認真處理工作一樣。
generatedExample: She gave a business-like answer and moved on quickly.

explanationZhTw: 務實的精明或機靈，帶點狡黠，表示她很會用實際方法解決問題。
generatedExample: Her practical cunning helped the group avoid trouble.

explanationZhTw: 徹底而完整的知識，沒有遺漏；這裡指她對周邊地區非常熟悉。
generatedExample: He has exhaustive knowledge of the subject.

explanationZhTw: 把某人帶到某個地方或出口，這裡指路線最後使他到達不同的火車站。
generatedExample: The shortcut brought us out near the bridge.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `hence` 是較正式的用法，表示「從現在起、之後」，整句是指「四個晚上後」或「四天後的某個晚上」取決於語境。
generatedExample: We will leave two days hence.

explanationZhTw: `quarters` 在這裡不是「四分之一」，而是指「地區、街區、城區的一部分」。
generatedExample: They live in the old quarter of the city.

explanationZhTw: `hang about` 是片語動詞，意思是「閒晃、逗留不走、在附近徘徊」。
generatedExample: We spent the afternoon hanging about in the park.

explanationZhTw: `in search of` 表示「尋找、為了找……」，是比較固定的表達。
generatedExample: He went into town in search of a new jacket.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，意思是「周圍沒有危險、沒有人在監視，可以放心行動」。
generatedExample: Wait until the coast is clear before you call me.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `recognition` 指「認出」的動作或結果，意思是他要裝作沒認出她。
generatedExample: She smiled in recognition when she saw her old teacher.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `due back` 指「預定／必須回來」，也就是她要在七點半回去，不是字面上的「欠回」。
generatedExample: I’m due back at the office by 4 p.m.

explanationZhTw: `put in` 在這裡表示「投入、花費（時間）」，強調她要拿出兩小時去做這件事。
generatedExample: She put in three extra hours to finish the report.

explanationZhTw: `handing out` 表示「分發、派發」，這裡是把傳單發給別人。
generatedExample: They were handing out free samples outside the station.

explanationZhTw: `leaflets` 是「傳單、宣傳單」，通常是薄薄一張用來宣傳或通知的紙張。
generatedExample: The volunteers left leaflets on every doorstep.

explanationZhTw: `bloody` 是英式粗口，這裡只是加強語氣，意思接近「該死的／真他媽的」，不是字面上的「流血的」。
generatedExample: It’s bloody cold today.

explanationZhTw: `brush-down` 在這裡指「幫我快速刷一刷／整理一下」，多半是把頭髮或衣服上的灰塵、碎屑弄掉。
generatedExample: He gave his jacket a quick brush-down before leaving.

explanationZhTw: 「flung herself into his arms」表示她很突然、很激動地投入他的懷抱，帶有強烈情感與快速動作的語氣。
generatedExample: After months apart, she flung herself into her brother's arms and started crying.

explanationZhTw: 「pushed her way through」是指用力擠過、推開阻礙前進，強調有阻力但仍硬要通過。
generatedExample: We pushed our way through the crowd to get near the stage.

explanationZhTw: 「found out」在這裡是「查出、得知」的意思，不是單純「找到」。
generatedExample: I finally found out why the meeting was canceled.

explanationZhTw: 「made no difference」表示「沒有差別、無所謂」，也就是前面的情況不影響結果。
generatedExample: It made no difference whether we left at six or seven.

explanationZhTw: 「inconceivable」表示幾乎無法想像、難以相信，在這裡是「根本不可能發生」的語氣。
generatedExample: It was inconceivable that the small village could support a new airport.

explanationZhTw: 「went back」是片語動詞，這裡表示「回到某個地方去」。
generatedExample: I went back to the shop to pick up my umbrella.

explanationZhTw: 「after a fashion」是慣用語，意思是「勉強地、算是、在某種程度上」。
generatedExample: The repair worked, after a fashion, but it still needs more work.

explanationZhTw: 「drifted down」在這裡不是字面上的「漂下去」，而是指兩人漫不經心地沿著人行道走。
generatedExample: We drifted down the river in silence and watched the lights.

explanationZhTw: 「carried on」是片語動詞，意思是「繼續進行、持續說下去」。
generatedExample: She carried on talking as if nothing had happened.

explanationZhTw: 「on and off」是慣用語，表示「斷斷續續地、時有時無地」。
generatedExample: It rained on and off all afternoon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「nipped into silence」是比喻用法，意思是談話被立刻、突然地打斷到安靜下來。
generatedExample: Our argument nipped into silence when the teacher walked in.

explanationZhTw: 「taken up again」是片語動詞，意思是「在中斷後重新開始」。
generatedExample: We took up the discussion again after lunch.

explanationZhTw: 「cut short」是片語動詞，表示「使突然停止、縮短」。
generatedExample: She cut short her speech when the phone rang.

explanationZhTw: 「be used to」表示「習慣於」，這裡指 Julia 對這種斷續的談話方式很習慣。
generatedExample: I am quite used to early mornings now.

explanationZhTw: 「talking by instalments」是作者自造的說法，意思是把談話分成一段一段、零碎地進行。
generatedExample: He described their meetings as talking by instalments because they only spoke for a minute at a time.

explanationZhTw: 「deathly white」是描述臉色的慣用說法，意思是「慘白得像死了一樣」，強調極度驚恐或受傷。
generatedExample: After hearing the news, his face turned deathly white.

explanationZhTw: 「rendezvous」指秘密會面地點或約會點，語氣比一般的 meeting 更正式，也帶一點隱密感。
generatedExample: They met at a riverside rendezvous after sunset.

explanationZhTw: 「come round」在這裡是「繞過來、轉過彎」的意思，表示巡邏隊突然出現在街角附近。
generatedExample: I froze when the security guard came round the corner.

explanationZhTw: 「camouflage」是「偽裝、掩護」；這裡指表面上做些事，實際上是為了掩蓋真正目的。
generatedExample: Her casual smile was just camouflage for her anxiety.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「mortgage」是比喻用法，不是指真的貸款抵押，而是指把晚上的時間「抵出去」或犧牲掉。
generatedExample: I had to mortgage yet another evening to finish the report.

explanationZhTw: 表示「零碎的、不完整的」；這裡指他們的談話斷斷續續、內容不連貫。
generatedExample: Her notes were fragmentary, so I had to reconstruct the full story.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，表示「補上、填補」；指把談話中的空白補起來。
generatedExample: A few friendly questions filled up the awkward silence.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「燃燒中的」，而是形容天氣「酷熱的、炙熱的」。
generatedExample: We walked only a few minutes before the blazing sun drove us inside.

explanationZhTw: 表示「停滯不流動的、悶滯的」；這裡指空氣不流通，聞起來又悶又臭。
generatedExample: The stagnant air in the basement made the room feel unpleasant.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，表示「匆匆瞥一眼、掃視一下」。
generatedExample: He cast a glance at his watch and hurried toward the station.

explanationZhTw: 指「箭孔、射箭用的狹長孔洞」；是城堡或塔樓常見的防禦設計。
generatedExample: The castle walls had narrow arrowslits for defense.

explanationZhTw: 指「旅社式宿舍／青年宿舍」；這裡是多人同住的住宿場所，不是飯店。
generatedExample: I stayed in a hostel near the station for two nights.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「女人的臭味」，這裡是 Julia 帶情緒的抱怨，表示她非常厭惡那種女性很多、擁擠又令人不快的環境。
generatedExample: He complained about the stink of cigarette smoke in the room.

explanationZhTw: 此處不是一般「寫小說的機器」的中性說法，而是小說中虛構的工作設備名稱，帶有諷刺意味：用機械大量生產文學。
generatedExample: The factory displayed old novel-writing machines as part of the exhibit.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「運轉並維護／保養」機器；`servicing` 在此不是一般「提供服務」，而是照料機械、讓它正常運作。
generatedExample: He spent the morning running and servicing the generator.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「對……很熟悉、很自在、很上手」。
generatedExample: After a week, I felt at home with the new software.

explanationZhTw: 指「最後修飾、潤飾、微調」；這裡是對作品做最後整理。
generatedExample: The designer spent an hour touching up the poster before printing.

explanationZhTw: 表示「不太喜歡、沒什麼興趣」。語氣比直接說 `didn't like` 更委婉。
generatedExample: I didn't much care for the movie.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「商品、可大量生產和交易的東西」；在語境中強調書被當成工業產品看待，而不是藝術作品。
generatedExample: In this market, water is treated as a commodity.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `borne` 不是常見的「承受」，而是較正式的「一直具有／保持」；整句是說她一直有很好的名聲與品行。
generatedExample: He had always borne a reputation for honesty.

explanationZhTw: `pick out` 這裡是片語動詞，意思是「挑選出來、特別選中」。
generatedExample: She was picked out for the lead role.

explanationZhTw: `turn out` 這裡不是「結果是」，而是「生產、製造」，指大量做出某種東西。
generatedExample: The factory turns out thousands of phones every day.

explanationZhTw: `proles` 是小說中的術語，來自 `proletarians`，指一般平民或無產階級。
generatedExample: The regime kept the proles under constant surveillance.

explanationZhTw: `furtively` 表示「偷偷摸摸地、鬼鬼祟祟地」，帶有怕被發現的意味。
generatedExample: He glanced furtively at the note on the desk.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，意思是「誤以為；以為自己知道某件事其實不對」。
generatedExample: I was under the impression that the meeting started at nine.

explanationZhTw: 「curiously」在這裡表示「帶著好奇心地、好奇地」；用來描述 Winston 說話時的態度，不是「奇怪地」的意思。
generatedExample: She asked curiously about the old photographs.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是很強烈的貶義說法，意思接近「糟透的胡說八道／爛東西」；`ghastly` 加強語氣，`rubbish` 在英式英語裡可表示「廢話、垃圾話」。
generatedExample: That was ghastly rubbish, and everyone knew it.

explanationZhTw: 指「無聊的、乏味的」。
generatedExample: The lecture was boring, so I left early.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，表示把順序對調、互換一下，或輪流換位置。
generatedExample: We can swap them round and see which version works best.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是對話中的強調語氣，意思接近「當然／那是自然」；不是字面上的「照著規律」。
generatedExample: Of course you can borrow my notes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `on` 不是「在上面」，而是指「負責／被分派到」某項工作或組別；整個說法帶有機構內部用語的感覺。
generatedExample: She was on the graphics team last month.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指「文學的」，而是說自己不屬於有文學修養、會寫作的人；語氣帶點自嘲。
generatedExample: He's very literary and loves modern poetry.

explanationZhTw: 「astonishment」表示「驚訝、震驚」，語氣比一般的 surprise 更強，表示他對這件事非常意外。
generatedExample: She stared at the results in astonishment.

explanationZhTw: 「heads of the departments」是指「各部門主管／部門負責人」，這裡的 head 不是「頭」，而是「領導者」的意思。
generatedExample: The heads of the departments met on Monday to discuss the budget.

explanationZhTw: 「sex instincts」指「性本能／性衝動」，是比較正式、帶有抽象分析感的說法。
generatedExample: The article discussed how sex instincts can influence behavior.

explanationZhTw: 「controllable」表示「可控制的」，這裡是在說這些本能比較難被約束。
generatedExample: The situation became more controllable after the new rules were introduced.

explanationZhTw: 「corrupted」在這裡不是單純的「損壞」，而是「被腐化、被不良內容影響而變壞」的意思。
generatedExample: Some people feared that the violent film would corrupt children.

explanationZhTw: 「filth」字面是「污物、骯髒東西」，但在這裡帶有貶抑意味，指被視為低俗、下流或令人厭惡的內容。
generatedExample: He refused to read what he called the filth in the magazine.

explanationZhTw: 「handled」是「處理、接觸、經手」的意思，這裡指他們在工作中接觸到那些材料。
generatedExample: The workers handled fragile glass with care.

explanationZhTw: 指「已婚婦女」；這裡是一般字面用法，沒有特別隱含意思。
generatedExample: The hotel does not allow married women to stay in the same dormitory.

explanationZhTw: 表示「被認為應該、被期待要」；這裡不是「假裝」，而是說社會對女孩有某種期待。
generatedExample: Children are supposed to be polite when they meet adults.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 pure 不是指「純淨的」材料，而是指「純潔的、清白的、沒有性經驗的」這種道德或性方面的意思。
generatedExample: People used to expect women to stay pure before marriage.

explanationZhTw: 表示「不管怎樣、無論如何」；用來補上一個帶有轉折或修正的語氣。
generatedExample: It may not be perfect, but it's useful anyway.

explanationZhTw: 指「戀愛關係」或帶有不太正式、甚至有點曖昧意味的「情事」。
generatedExample: Their love-affair ended after they moved to different cities.

explanationZhTw: 指「玩得開心、享樂」，不是單指「很好的時間點」。
generatedExample: We had a good time at the concert last night.

explanationZhTw: 指「稍微提到、涉及到」。這裡表示只要和她自己的生活有關，她才會在意。
generatedExample: The speaker touched upon climate policy but did not go into detail.

explanationZhTw: 指「逐漸變成日常會用的普通詞彙」，不是字面上的「走進」某處。
generatedExample: Several technical terms have passed into everyday use.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `remote` 不是指地理上的「偏遠」，而是指「太渺茫、太不可能」。
generatedExample: The chance of getting a refund now feels remote.

explanationZhTw: `sanction` 這裡是「批准、正式允許」，不是常見的「制裁」意思。
generatedExample: The manager would not sanction the plan without more evidence.

explanationZhTw: `get rid of` 是片語動詞，意思是「擺脫、除掉、設法處理掉」。這裡帶有把障礙消除的意思。
generatedExample: I need to get rid of these old files before moving.

explanationZhTw: `What was she like` 這裡的 `like` 不是「喜歡」，而是用來詢問某人的外貌、性格或整體印象。
generatedExample: What was your new boss like?

explanationZhTw: `wife` 是「妻子」；指一個已婚男性的配偶。
generatedExample: My wife is a doctor.

explanationZhTw: 《Newspeak》是《1984》中的官方新語，目的在縮減詞彙、限制人們用語言表達反抗或獨立思考。
generatedExample: The party kept adding Newspeak words to narrow what people could say.

explanationZhTw: 《GOODTHINKFUL》是書中捏造的 Newspeak 詞，意思是完全符合官方思想、沒有壞念頭。
generatedExample: A GOODTHINKFUL citizen never questions the party line.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的《orthodox》不是宗教上的「正統」，而是指思想或立場完全符合官方標準。
generatedExample: His orthodox response matched exactly what the manager expected.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `word` 是指「字、詞、說法」，也就是某個表達或用語；不是「話語」的抽象意義。
generatedExample: I know the word, but I still need to check its exact meaning.

explanationZhTw: `right enough` 是英式口語用法，表示「確實、沒錯、的確如此」，帶有加強語氣。
generatedExample: He was worried, right enough, but he finished the job.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「說來奇怪／令人驚訝的是」，用來指出後面的情況和預期不一樣。
generatedExample: Curiously enough, the answer was already written on the board.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指「變硬」，而是指身體因緊張、不舒服或抗拒而變得僵硬。
generatedExample: I could feel the stiffening of his shoulders when I mentioned the topic.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示雙手很用力地環抱著他，強調「緊緊抓住不放」的動作。
generatedExample: She held the child clasped tightly round her waist.

explanationZhTw: 表示「無論如何／反正」，常用來補充或轉回重點，意思接近 anyway。
generatedExample: We are late, but in any case the meeting has not started yet.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「令人反感的、讓人不舒服的」，比單純的「不好吃」更偏向情緒或道德上的厭惡。
generatedExample: He found the comment distasteful and decided not to reply.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `stood it` 不是字面上的「站著承受」，而是「忍受、受得了」。整句是在說：如果不是因為某件事，他本來還可以忍耐。
generatedExample: I could stand it for an hour, but not all day.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `frigid` 不是指天氣冷，而是指「冷淡、沒有感情、令人不舒服」；`ceremony` 也不是正式典禮，而是帶有固定程序、很惱人的例行儀式。
generatedExample: The interview felt like a frigid little ceremony with no real warmth.

explanationZhTw: `forced` 表示「強迫、逼迫」。這裡是說 Katharine 逼他每週都得做那件事。
generatedExample: My parents forced me to finish my homework before dinner.

explanationZhTw: `go through` 在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「經歷、不得不接受」某件通常不愉快的事。
generatedExample: We had to go through a lot of paperwork before we could move in.

explanationZhTw: 「duty」指義務、責任。這裡是指對政黨的忠誠義務。
generatedExample: She sees it as her duty to help her neighbors.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「Party」不是一般的「派對」，而是指《1984》裡的極權執政黨「黨」。
generatedExample: In the novel, the Party controls every part of daily life.

explanationZhTw: 指「16歲以上的人」。這裡把年齡層當名詞用，表示特定年齡組。
generatedExample: The swimming pool is open to over-sixteens after 6 p.m.

explanationZhTw: 指反覆向你灌輸某種觀念，講很多次到讓人覺得煩。
generatedExample: My teacher rubbed it into us for weeks that deadlines matter.

explanationZhTw: 表示「我想／大概／恐怕」，語氣帶點保留，屬於較書面或稍舊式的說法。
generatedExample: I dare say she is right about the timing.

explanationZhTw: 指「偽君子」或「口是心非的人」，表面上一套、實際上又是一套。
generatedExample: He said the committee was full of hypocrites who never followed their own rules.

explanationZhTw: "enlarge upon" 指「詳細闡述、展開說明」，不是字面上的「放大」。這裡表示她開始把主題講得更深入。
generatedExample: Could you enlarge upon your main concern in the meeting?

explanationZhTw: "came back to" 在此是「又回到、重新聚焦於」的意思，表示話題或思緒總會轉回這個點。
generatedExample: The discussion came back to cost in the end.

explanationZhTw: "touch upon" 表示「略微提到、輕觸某個話題」，不是實際接觸。
generatedExample: The speaker touched upon the problem but saved the details for later.

explanationZhTw: 這裡 "grasped" 不是「抓住」，而是「理解、領會」。
generatedExample: He grasped the point after one explanation.

explanationZhTw: "puritanism" 指在道德或性方面非常嚴格、保守的態度；"sexual puritanism" 就是「性方面的清教式禁慾與壓抑」。
generatedExample: The essay criticizes the sexual puritanism of the era.

explanationZhTw: "privation" 是「缺乏、匱乏」；"sexual privation" 指性被剝奪或長期壓抑的狀態。
generatedExample: Years of privation left the family exhausted.

explanationZhTw: "hysteria" 是「歇斯底里、異常激動的情緒失控」；這裡指被壓抑後引發的集體狂熱。
generatedExample: The announcement triggered hysteria in the crowd.

explanationZhTw: "war-fever" 是比喻說法，指「戰爭狂熱、對戰爭的亢奮支持」。
generatedExample: Propaganda fed war-fever across the country.

explanationZhTw: "leader-worship" 指「領袖崇拜」，對領袖的盲目崇敬與服從。
generatedExample: The regime depended on leader-worship to stay in power.

explanationZhTw: 指「做愛／發生性行為」的委婉說法，這裡不是字面上的「做愛」。
generatedExample: They made love after the wedding.

explanationZhTw: 指「把……用掉、消耗掉、用光」。
generatedExample: Running every day is using up my energy.

explanationZhTw: 指「完全不在乎、毫不在意」，通常帶有口語或粗俗語氣。
generatedExample: He doesn't give a damn what people think.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 bear 不是「熊」，而是「忍受、容忍」；can't bear you to feel like that 就是「受不了你有那種感覺」。
generatedExample: I can't bear loud noises.

explanationZhTw: 指「充滿活力、精力旺盛」，像要溢出來一樣。
generatedExample: The kids were bursting with energy after lunch.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「變酸了」，這裡引申為「變質、走味、變得不對勁」。
generatedExample: Their friendship went sour after the argument.

explanationZhTw: 英式粗俗說法；bloody 是加強語氣，rot 在這裡是「胡說、廢話、垃圾」。
generatedExample: That's bloody rot, and you know it.

explanationZhTw: 此處指「密切而內在的關聯」。
generatedExample: There was an intimate connexion between the two events.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是一般宗教用語，而是指「政治上的正統立場」或官方正確思想。
generatedExample: The journalist was punished for challenging the political orthodoxy.

explanationZhTw: 表示「維持在剛好的強度／程度」。
generatedExample: The music should be loud enough to energize people, but not so loud that it feels aggressive; it needs to stay at the right pitch.

explanationZhTw: 指「壓抑、壓住」某種情緒或本能。
generatedExample: He kept bottling down his anger until he finally exploded.

explanationZhTw: 表示「加以利用、轉為己用」。
generatedExample: She turned her hobby to account by selling her paintings online.

explanationZhTw: 指「使某人反感、敵視」某人或某事。
generatedExample: The rumor turned the crowd against the candidate.

explanationZhTw: 表示「暗中監視、刺探」。
generatedExample: The company installed cameras to spy on employees.

explanationZhTw: 此處是「偏離規範／正統的行為」，不是幾何中的偏差。
generatedExample: Any deviation from the rules was treated as suspicious.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 extension 不是「延伸物」的字面意思，而是指「變成某機構的一部分／附屬工具」。
generatedExample: The app became an extension of the school’s monitoring system.

explanationZhTw: 指「告密者、線民」，不是單純的資訊提供者。
generatedExample: The regime relied on informers to report any sign of dissent.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，意思不是「走回去」，而是「思緒回到／想起」凱瑟琳。
generatedExample: My mind went back to the trip we took last summer.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「公開指控、告發」，語氣很強，通常帶有向權威檢舉別人的意思。
generatedExample: He denounced the fraud to the authorities.

explanationZhTw: 這是小說中的專有名詞，指監視人們思想、查禁異端想法的國家機構。
generatedExample: In the story, the Thought Police watch people for signs of disloyalty.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「不符合官方正統／異端」的想法，不是一般日常的「不尋常」。
generatedExample: His unorthodoxy made him unpopular with the committee.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「悶熱得令人難受的」，常用來形容天氣或空氣。
generatedExample: The room felt stifling after the windows were closed.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「酷熱的、熱得難受的」，比一般的 hot 更強。
generatedExample: We stayed inside during the sweltering afternoon.

explanationZhTw: 表示「迷路、走錯路」。
generatedExample: We lost our way in the city after sunset.

explanationZhTw: 表示「落後於其他人、跟不上隊伍」。
generatedExample: I lagged behind on the climb, so I stopped to catch my breath.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「轉錯彎、走錯方向」；turning 在此是英式英語中的「轉彎處」。
generatedExample: We took a wrong turning and ended up near the river.

explanationZhTw: 表示「突然停住、猛然停下來」。
generatedExample: She pulled up short when she saw the loose wire.

explanationZhTw: 表示「問路、詢問怎麼走」。
generatedExample: We asked the way to the museum at the front desk.

explanationZhTw: 表示「不安、心裡發毛」。
generatedExample: He felt uneasy before the test results came back.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是法律上的「犯罪」，而是「覺得自己做錯事、良心不安」的感受。
generatedExample: Skipping the meeting gave her a strange feeling of wrong-doing.

explanationZhTw: 表示「趕快回去」。
generatedExample: We had to hurry back before the train left.

explanationZhTw: `look at` 是常見片語動詞，意思是「看、注意看」。這裡是在叫對方把視線移到花上。
generatedExample: Look at the map before you start walking.

explanationZhTw: `clump` 在這裡不是「塊、團」的抽象意思，而是指「一叢、一簇」花或植物，屬於語境中的特殊用法。
generatedExample: A clump of yellow flowers grew by the fence.

explanationZhTw: `come back` 在這裡是「回來、折返」；指她又回到原處一下，不是字面上的「再來一次」。「fretfully」只是修飾她回頭時不太耐煩的態度。
generatedExample: She came back to the desk to pick up her keys.

explanationZhTw: `leaned out` 表示「把身體向外探出」；這裡是她探身往懸崖外看。
generatedExample: He leaned out of the window to call his dog.

explanationZhTw: `steady her` 在這裡是「扶住她、讓她站穩」；`steady` 作動詞不是「穩定局勢」，而是幫人保持平衡。
generatedExample: She held the rail to steady herself on the stairs.

explanationZhTw: `occurred to him` 表示「他突然想到／意識到」；不是事情「發生在他身上」，而是某個念頭忽然進入腦中。
generatedExample: It occurred to me that I had left the oven on.

explanationZhTw: `human creature` 是文學或帶古典感的說法，意思就是「人、任何人」；這裡用來強調附近完全沒有人的蹤影。
generatedExample: Not a human creature was in sight after sunset.

explanationZhTw: `pick up` 在這裡是「接收、拾取到」聲音訊號；指麥克風只能把聲音錄進去。
generatedExample: My phone can pick up a weak signal in this room.

explanationZhTw: `sleepiest hour` 指一天中最容易昏昏欲睡、最靜悄悄的時段；這裡是在營造午後慵懶、沉滯的氣氛。
generatedExample: The sleepiest hour of the day is usually right after lunch.

explanationZhTw: `blazed down` 指太陽「猛烈地灼照下來」；不是一般的照射，而是強烈到像在燃燒。
generatedExample: The sun blazed down on the empty field all afternoon.

explanationZhTw: `struck him` 在這裡是「他突然想到／一下子意識到」；不是物理上的撞擊，而是念頭突然閃現。
generatedExample: It struck him that he had forgotten her birthday.

explanationZhTw: `shove` 是「用力推、猛推」的意思；`give someone a shove` 表示「狠狠推某人一下」。這裡是直接的動作描述，不是比喻用法。
generatedExample: He gave the box a shove to move it out of the way.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `would have` 表示對過去情況的假設回應，意思接近「當時就會／本來會那樣」，不是單純的過去式。
generatedExample: I would have told you earlier if I had known.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「同一個人」，而是指「如果當時的我和現在的我一樣」，強調性格、立場或自我認同沒有改變。
generatedExample: I might have acted differently if I had been the same person then as I am now.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `sorry` 不是「抱歉」的道歉用法，而是表示「後悔、遺憾」。整句是在問：你會不會後悔自己沒有那樣做？
generatedExample: She was sorry she didn't tell him the truth earlier.

explanationZhTw: 「on the whole」表示「整體來說、總的來看」，用來概括全部情況，不是指某個單一部分。
generatedExample: On the whole, the meeting went well, even though we ran out of time at the end.

explanationZhTw: 表示「並排、肩並肩」；這裡是很常見的方位片語，屬於 A2 以上就應該認得的基礎用法。
generatedExample: We sat side by side during the movie.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「征服」的字面意思，而是指香味「壓過、蓋過」難聞的味道；屬於情境中的比喻用法。
generatedExample: The strong smell of coffee conquered the odor from the trash can.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「不方便的人」不是日常字面意思，而是帶有政治或權力語境的委婉說法，指某個人對當權者來說是礙事、需要被排除的人。
generatedExample: In the report, he was described as an inconvenient person who had to be silenced.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Actually` 不是在表示「實際地做了某事」，而是表示「其實、說真的」，用來修正或補充前面的意思。
generatedExample: Actually, I think we should wait until tomorrow.

explanationZhTw: `make no difference` 是常見表達，意思是「沒有差別、無關緊要」。這句是過去完成式條件語氣，表示「就算那樣做，也不會有任何影響」。
generatedExample: It would have made no difference if we had arrived earlier.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `sorry` 不是單純「對不起」的道歉語氣，而是表示「懊悔、遺憾、後悔」：因為沒做那件事而感到不安。
generatedExample: She was sorry she didn't call him back sooner.

explanationZhTw: 「prefer」表示「較喜歡、偏好」，這裡是很常見的比較用法，表示在兩者之中選擇較好的一個。
generatedExample: I prefer tea to coffee in the morning.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「positive」不是指「正面的性格」，而是指「較有利、較好的結果／一面」。屬於依上下文延伸的意思。
generatedExample: She tried to focus on the positive side of the situation.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「negative」不是單純的文法或數學用法，而是指「不利的、較差的那一面」。
generatedExample: He always notices the negative parts of a plan first.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「game」不是字面上的遊戲，而是比喻某種鬥爭、角力或局勢中的互動。
generatedExample: In this political game, every move matters.

explanationZhTw: 「win」是常見動詞，意思是「贏得、獲勝」。這裡用在比喻語境中，表示不可能取得理想結果。
generatedExample: If we keep arguing, nobody will win.

explanationZhTw: 「failure」是「失敗」；在這裡偏向抽象名詞，指某種結果或處境上的失敗。
generatedExample: Failure can teach you more than success sometimes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `dissent` 是「反對、異議」；`wriggle` 在此不是單純指扭動，而是帶有「細微地表達不贊同」的意思。
generatedExample: She gave a small wriggle of dissent when the plan was mentioned.

explanationZhTw: `contradicted` 表示「反駁；和某人意見相反」。
generatedExample: My brother contradicted me during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `a law of nature` 字面是「自然法則」，這裡是指「像自然定律一樣不可改變的真理」。
generatedExample: He treated failure as a law of nature, but I disagreed.

explanationZhTw: `doomed` 表示「注定失敗的；難逃厄運的」。
generatedExample: Without support, the project was doomed from the start.

explanationZhTw: `sooner or later` 是固定說法，表示「遲早；終究」。
generatedExample: Sooner or later, the truth will come out.

explanationZhTw: `Thought Police` 是小說中的專有名詞，指監控思想與言論的政治警察或秘密警察。
generatedExample: In the novel, the Thought Police punish even private thoughts.

explanationZhTw: `construct` 在這裡是「建構、設計出」；常比 `build` 更正式，也可指抽象事物。
generatedExample: They tried to construct a better system for the team.

explanationZhTw: `cunning` 指「狡猾而機靈的；善於計謀的」，帶有能想辦法達成目的的意味。
generatedExample: She used cunning to escape the difficult situation.

explanationZhTw: `boldness` 是「大膽；勇敢」；這裡強調做事時敢於冒險。
generatedExample: His boldness impressed everyone in the room.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `dead` 不是單純指「已經死亡的人」，而是帶有比喻意味，表示「我們已經註定完蛋、沒有活路了」。
generatedExample: After the company lost its main client, the project was dead.

explanationZhTw: 「prosaically」表示「平實地、毫不矯飾地、很務實地」；在這裡是在說茱莉亞說話的語氣很冷靜、沒有浪漫化或戲劇化。
generatedExample: She answered prosaically, listing the facts without any drama.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「在身體層面上」；和後面的死亡恐懼相比，是在區分身體上的狀態，而不是情緒或心理層面。
generatedExample: She was hurt emotionally, but not physically.

explanationZhTw: 表示「按理說有可能、也許可以想像」；語氣比 probably 更弱，帶有勉強可成立的推測感。
generatedExample: Conceivably, the train could arrive late because of the storm.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「據推測、想必」；說話者是根據年齡做推論，不是在陳述確定事實。
generatedExample: Presumably, she already knows the answer.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「把事情往後延、拖延」；常用來表示延後處理某件事。
generatedExample: We decided to put it off until next week.

explanationZhTw: 指「人類、人的個體」；比單說 humans 稍正式，也常用來強調人類這個物種。
generatedExample: Human beings need connection and purpose.

explanationZhTw: `rubbish` 在這裡是英式口語，意思是「胡說、廢話、不可信的說法」，不是字面上的「垃圾」。
generatedExample: That's rubbish; nobody can finish it that fast.

explanationZhTw: `would you sooner` 這裡不是「更早」，而是口語中的偏好說法，意思接近 `would you rather`，表示「你比較想要哪一個」。
generatedExample: Would you sooner leave now or wait until morning?

explanationZhTw: `sleep with` 在這裡是委婉語，意思是「和某人發生性關係」，不是單純「一起睡覺」。
generatedExample: He admitted he had slept with someone he worked with.

explanationZhTw: 「twisted herself round」表示把身體轉過來、扭身轉向某人。這裡是一般動作描述，屬於口語中常見的片語動詞/動作片語用法。
generatedExample: She twisted herself round to look at the door.

explanationZhTw: 「bosom」指胸部或胸口，屬於較文學、較古典的說法；在這裡不是一般日常口語的「胸懷」意思。
generatedExample: She clutched the letter to her bosom.

explanationZhTw: 「ripe」原本常指水果成熟，這裡是比喻用法，表示身體已發育成熟、帶有性感或成熟女性的意味。
generatedExample: The novel uses the word ripe to suggest mature, full-grown beauty.

explanationZhTw: 「vigour」指活力、精力、生命力；這裡用在較文學的語境中，表示一種強烈的身體能量。
generatedExample: After the walk, he felt full of vigour.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `fix up about` 指「商量／安排好」下一次見面的事，屬於口語說法。
generatedExample: Let's fix up about lunch next week.

explanationZhTw: `may as well` 表示「不妨／索性」，意思是做這件事也沒什麼壞處。
generatedExample: We may as well leave now.

explanationZhTw: `go back to` 在這裡是「回到」某個地方。
generatedExample: We went back to the hotel after dinner.

explanationZhTw: `planned out` 表示「已經整個規劃好了、安排妥當」。
generatedExample: She had the whole trip planned out.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「拉長」或「畫出」，而是指把路線或地點示意畫給對方看。
generatedExample: I can draw it out for you on this map.

explanationZhTw: 「scraped together」在這裡不是字面上真的刮在一起，而是指「勉強湊集、費力收集到」一些東西，帶有費勁、數量不多的感覺。
generatedExample: She scraped together enough money for a bus ticket.

explanationZhTw: 「twig」在這裡是指「細樹枝、樹枝條」，不是一般較常見的抽象用法。
generatedExample: He picked up a twig and used it to point at the path.
